summary.what: Order commit→log→push explicitly; warn timbers log when pushed before logged; extract protocol text to internal/protocol
summary.why: Push-before-log race in osprey-strike stranded an entry locally — protocol said 'commit, log' but didn't bold the no-push-between-them rule, and timbers log gave no signal even though the data to detect it was sitting right there
summary.how: IsPushedToUpstream in internal/git checks the docs anchor against @{u} after WriteEntry; printer.Warn fires when the documented commit is already on upstream but the entry isn't yet. Protocol text rewrote ordering with a 'never push between' callout. Shared protocol/stale-anchor sections moved into internal/protocol package, composed by both cmd/timbers (full PRIME doc) and internal/mcp (subset)

notes: Considered single-file approach for protocol text (skip-authors via prefix in .timbersignore inspired this) but decided the workflow text is fundamentally compositional — different consumers need different subsets. Const+const concatenation in Go gives compile-time composition without runtime concat overhead. Reviewer initially missed that the empty-glob author case was already handled by the malformed-glob silent drop; added explicit ordering-position assertion to the protocol sanity test after the reviewer pointed out the prose-only check would miss a reordered checklist.
